title: 15. AI Presence and Overlays
description: A tour of the AI Presence and Companion Orb addon tabs and how they relate to the main thinking/speaking visual state.

steps.title: Open AI Presence
steps.body: AI Presence controls the fullscreen and floating visualizer used while NC thinks or speaks. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step in this tutorial panel if you want spoken instructions while you inspect these controls.

steps.title: Choose Display Mode
steps.body: Display decides whether AI Presence is off, fullscreen, floating, or both. Start with Floating when you want a small persistent indicator, and use Fullscreen only when you want NC to take over the screen during thinking or speech.

steps.title: Preview Without Starting Chat
steps.body: Show Fullscreen and Show Floating let you check the visualizer without starting a chat reply. Reset Floating Position recenters the floating window when it ends up off-screen or behind other windows.

steps.title: Add Subtle Idle Motion
steps.body: Idle motion adds a small random drift around the presence position so it feels alive without racing around the screen. Keep this low for release demos, and set it to zero when you want the visualizer perfectly still.

steps.title: Use External Runtime When Needed
steps.body: External runtime runs the presence renderer in a separate helper process. Use it when animation feels laggy in the main UI, and turn it off only when you are diagnosing renderer startup or bridge behavior.

steps.title: Companion Orb Has Its Own Tab
steps.body: Companion Orb is the small desktop orb. It has separate controls for overlay display, movement, target capture, supervisor behavior, and hotkeys.

steps.title: Use Orb Targeting Deliberately
steps.body: The Companion Orb sensory tabs decide whether the orb can feed selected-window, selected-region, or full-screen context into hidden sensory feedback. Keep broad full-screen context off until you actually want desktop-wide awareness.
